summary: PR 227 reorganized camera-related assembly by renaming asm/melee/cm/code_80028B9C.s to asm/melee/cm/camera.s and splitting the cmsnap-specific block at 0x800315C8-0x80031720 into a new asm/melee/cm/cmsnap.s object. It moved the associated cmsnap assert strings and BSS symbol lbl_80453060 with that block, then updated obj_files.mk to build camera.o and cmsnap.o instead of the former monolithic code_80028B9C.o. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Renamed asm/melee/cm/code_80028B9C.s to asm/melee/cm/camera.s with high similarity retained.
Removed the cmsnap assembly block from camera.s, including func_800315C8, func_80031618, local routine lbl_80031640, and func_800316B4.
Created asm/melee/cm/cmsnap.s containing the extracted text block at addresses 0x800315C8 through 0x80031720.
Moved cmsnap-specific data strings into cmsnap.s: lbl_803BCD90 containing "cmsnap.c" and lbl_803BCD9C containing "_p(status) == CmSnapStatus_Sleep".
Moved cmsnap-specific BSS allocation lbl_80453060, size 0x20, from camera.s into cmsnap.s.
Updated obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES uses $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/cm/camera.o and $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/cm/cmsnap.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/cm/code_80028B9C.o.
Adjusted the remaining camera.s BSS section directive from `.section .bss, "wa"` to `.section .bss` after the cmsnap BSS block was removed.
smart_moves: Used the embedded source filename string "cmsnap.c" as evidence that the extracted routines and data belonged in a separate cmsnap assembly file.
Kept related text, assert rodata/data, and BSS together in cmsnap.s, reducing the risk of later decompilation work separating a function from its source-file-local globals.
Preserved original symbol names and addresses while reorganizing file boundaries, which is a low-risk way to improve object granularity before full C decompilation.
Updated obj_files.mk in the same change as the split, ensuring the build order/object list reflected the new object layout.
decomp_lessons: When a monolithic asm file contains an assert source string such as "cmsnap.c", that string can be strong evidence for carving out a source-file-sized assembly unit.
When splitting assembly files, move the associated literal strings and BSS storage with the functions that reference them, not just the text section.
Object-file boundary cleanup can be valuable even without converting code to C, because it gives future matching work smaller, source-named units.
Build list updates are part of an asm split: the old object must be removed or replaced with every new object required to preserve the linked binary layout.
naming_conventions: Renamed generic address-derived assembly file code_80028B9C.s to subsystem/source-style camera.s.
Introduced cmsnap.s based on the embedded original filename string "cmsnap.c".
Preserved unknown function labels such as func_800315C8, func_80031618, and func_800316B4 rather than inventing semantic names not proven by the diff.
Preserved anonymous/local labels like lbl_80031640 and data labels like lbl_803BCD90, lbl_803BCD9C, and lbl_80453060.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split an existing assembly object by physically moving a contiguous text range into a new .s file while keeping addresses and instruction bytes unchanged.
Moved the assert strings referenced by the extracted code into the new object to maintain source-file locality.
Moved the BSS symbol lbl_80453060 into the new object with its users, preserving its 0x20-byte allocation.
Updated TEXT_O_FILES order around the cm subsystem so camera.o is followed by cmsnap.o, replacing the prior single code_80028B9C.o entry.
Kept `.include "macros.inc"` and explicit section declarations in the new cmsnap.s file to match the style of existing asm files.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that decompile asm/melee/cm/cmsnap.s or rename func_800315C8, func_80031618, or func_800316B4.
Search for subsequent camera subsystem splits that use embedded source filename strings to identify object boundaries.
Check later changes to lbl_80453060 to see whether its 0x20-byte BSS allocation became a named cmsnap status/state struct.
